metadata.title:
ORDER OF MARCH FOR


LABOR DAY PARADE

pages.raw_text:
The Labor Day committee appoint
ed by the trades council to arrange
for the big celebration on Monday
next held a meeting East night and
completed many of the plans.


The celebration is to be a big af-
fair, calling out all the local unions
in a monster parade, after which all
hands will repair to the Natatorium
for a day of pleasure. There will be
speaking by J. r. Sovereign, c. r.
Zienke, Fred n. Foss and C. A Fos-
ter, after which sports and dancing
will be engaged in.


The colored Baptists of the city
will give big barbecue on the
grounds and there will be several
show attractions. for which privi-
leges have been let.


The marchers in the parade will
move four abreast, under the leader
ship of Grand Marshal w. D. Jack
man. There will be many floats of
beautiful and original pattern. The
order of the unions in line and line
OF march follows:


Section Marshal Of the day. w
D. Jackman; platoon Of police
Hopes band, Spokane trades council
building laborers' council, speakers Ir
carriages. typographical union, tail
ors. plasterers. building laborers
plumbers, bricklayers, stone masons
letter carriers. granite cutters, cleo
trical workers; division aide, Teddie
Reynolds.


Section 2-Carpenters and joiners
